titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Andover Newton Theological School Theses Collection
Andover Newton Theological School Theses Collection
titlestmt.author: Divinity Library staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 2, 2019 at 23:00

did.unittitle: Andover Newton Theological School Theses Collection
did.abstract: This collection includes theses from Andover Newton Theological School, and its predecessors Andover Theological Seminary and Newton Theological Institution.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The theses are arranged by date and then by author under each of the three institutions represented. Authors are listed individual except for boxes 3-30 under Newton Theological Institution where a range of authors for each is provided.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Andover Newton Theological School was established in 1965 by the merger of Andover Theological Seminary (established 1807) and Newton Theological Institution (established 1825). While maintaining a campus in Newton through 2018 and granting degrees through 2019, it formally affiliated with Yale Divinity School in 2017 and established a presence in New Haven as Andover Newton Seminary at Yale Divinity School.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: From the Andover Newton Theological School Collection, transferred to Yale Divinity Library in 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection includes theses written by students at Andover Newton Theological School and its predecessors, Andover Theological Seminary and Newton Theological Institution, which merged to form Andover Newton Theological School in 1965. The theses were written for degrees of various types, including Ph.D.,D.Min., M.A., S.T.M., and M.A.T.S.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Andover Newton Theological School Theses Collection (RG 300), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library
